mnemonics: Going Downhill Rapidly Every Day, Calculating The Steepest Route
keyPoints: An iterative method to find the minimum of a function by moving in the direction opposite to the gradient.
The step size is controlled by the learning rate parameter.
It is guaranteed to converge to a global minimum for convex functions and a local minimum for non-convex functions.
commonMistakes: Setting the learning rate too high (causes overshooting) or too low (causes slow convergence).
Forgetting to normalize or scale features, which can make the cost function highly elliptical and slow down convergence.
